title: Breach Discovery ‚Äî Time-to-Detect from First PHI Indicator

compliance.assurance_rationale: HIPAA Security ¬ß164.404 ‚Äî Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that HIPAA Security ¬ß164.404 is enforced ‚Äî Splunk UC-22.10.43: Breach Discovery ‚Äî Time-to-Detect from First PHI Indicator.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.10.43' running on notable (_time, rule_name, status), index=dlp sourcetype=ms:o365:management or ms:o365:dlp (IncidentId, CreatedTime), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted ‚Äî SME review required.

spl:
`notable` HIPAA_Breach_Candidate=1 earliest=-90d
| eval detect_time=_time
| join IncidentId type=left [
    search index=dlp sourcetype="o365:dlp" earliest=-90d
    | eval first_indicator=strptime(CreatedTime,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
    | stats min(first_indicator) as first_dlp by IncidentId ]
| eval hours_to_detect=round((detect_time-first_dlp)/3600,2)
| table IncidentId, first_dlp, detect_time, hours_to_detect, rule_name, status
| sort - hours_to_detect
description: Measures elapsed time between earliest technical indicator of a potential PHI incident and formal incident creation so discovery timelines support Breach Notification Rule evidence.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for HIPAA Security compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes dlp and sourcetypes ms:o365:management`, o365:dlp. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Tag correlation searches that create HIPAA candidate notables; (2) normalize UTC; (3) executive dashboard for legal discovery SLA. Indexes required: `index=dlp`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=ms:o365:management``, `sourcetype=o365:dlp`.
visualization: Histogram (hours_to_detect), Table (incidents), Single value (median detect hours).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern ‚Äî correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `` `notable` `` (`_time`, `rule_name`, `status`), `index=dlp` `sourcetype=ms:o365:management` or `ms:o365:dlp` (`IncidentId`, `CreatedTime`).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Tag correlation searches that create HIPAA candidate notables; (2) normalize UTC; (3) executive dashboard for legal discovery SLA.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
`notable` HIPAA_Breach_Candidate=1 earliest=-90d
| eval detect_time=_time
| join IncidentId type=left [
    search index=dlp sourcetype="o365:dlp" earliest=-90d
    | eval first_indicator=strptime(CreatedTime,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
    | stats min(first_indicator) as first_dlp by IncidentId ]
| eval hours_to_detect=round((detect_time-first_dlp)/3600,2)
| table IncidentId, first_dlp, detect_time, hours_to_detect, rule_name, status
| sort - hours_to_detect
```

Understanding this SPL

**Breach Discovery ‚Äî Time-to-Detect from First PHI Indicator (¬ß164.404)** ‚Äî Measures elapsed time between earliest technical indicator of a potential PHI incident and formal incident creation so discovery timelines support Breach Notification Rule evidence.

Documented **Data sources**: `` `notable` `` (`_time`, `rule_name`, `status`), `index=dlp` `sourcetype=ms:o365:management` or `ms:o365:dlp` (`IncidentId`, `CreatedTime`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Invokes macro `notable` ‚Äî in Search, use the UI or expand to inspect the underlying SPL.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **detect_time** ‚Äî applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
‚Ä¢ Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **hours_to_detect** ‚Äî applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.

‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Output fields: IncidentId, ], detect_time, first_dlp, first_indicator, hours_to_detect, rule_name, status.

Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Histogram (hours_to_detect), Table (incidents), Single value (median detect hours).
grandmaExplanation: We watch for measures elapsed time between earliest technical indicator of a potential phi incident and formal incident creation so discovery timelines support breach notification rule evidence. This is critical because gaps here could mean serious regulatory exposure or security blind spots.
